overview: Boros Prowess leverages cheap, resilient creatures to apply rapid early pressure while maintaining board control. Soul-Scar Mage amplifies Prowess triggers to accelerate damage output, while Emberheart Challenger provides a versatile threat that rewards combat. Screaming Nemesis offers late-game value and board presence to close out games when the early tempo advantage holds.
win_conditions: Screaming Nemesis trading up to stabilize and close out

keep_criteria: At least one cheap Prowess creature

early_game: Cast Soul-Scar Mage to pump attackers
mid_game: Deploy Emberheart Challenger to swing combat
Use Prowess triggers to dodge removal
late_game: Cast Screaming Nemesis for board presence
Use Nemesis to trade up or protect key cards
Close out with accumulated Prowess damage

key_cards.name: Screaming Nemesis
key_cards.notes: Hold until you have a target to trade or need a resilient blocker; use its pump ability to enable Prowess triggers.

key_cards.name: Emberheart Challenger
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and combat engine
key_cards.notes: Cast before attacking to maximize its enter-the-battlefield trigger; use it to swing wide and apply pressure.

key_cards.name: Soul-Scar Mage
key_cards.notes: Cast early to pump Prowess creatures; use its -1/-1 counter to dodge removal or boost damage output.

sequencing_tips: Hold Soul-Scar Mage until you have a Prowess creature to pump
Cast Emberheart Challenger before attacking to maximize its enter-the-battlefield trigger
Use Screaming Nemesis to trade up or protect key threats

matchups.advice: Prioritize early Prowess creatures and Soul-Scar Mage to outpace burn spells.
Use Screaming Nemesis to clear the board and stabilize against token swarms.
Apply pressure quickly before they establish a board with big threats.
